title: ATP Service Publish & Update
description: Publishing a new service to the ATP registry and updating an existing service registration
publishService_flow: → NO: return { ok: false, error: 'no_hub_url' } → END

STEP: build request body { sender_id: nodeId, title, description, capabilities, use_cases, price_per_task (min=1, default=10), execution_mode (default='exclusive'), max_concurrent (min=1, default=3), recipe_id }
STEP: POST fetch(endpoint, { method:'POST', headers, body: JSON.stringify(body), signal: AbortSignal.timeout(timeout) })
→ YES (catch): return { ok: false, error: err.message } → END
→ NO: read res.text() → return { ok: false, status: res.status, error: text.slice(0,400) } → END
→ YES: parse res.json() → return { ok: true, data } → END
updateService_flow: → NO: return { ok: false, error: 'no_hub_url' } → END
STEP: build request body { sender_id: nodeId, listing_id: listingId, ...updates (spread) }
STEP: POST fetch(endpoint, { method:'POST', headers, body: JSON.stringify(body), signal: AbortSignal.timeout(timeout) })
→ YES (catch): return { ok: false, error: err.message } → END
→ NO: read res.text() → return { ok: false, status: res.status, error: text.slice(0,400) } → END
→ YES: parse res.json() → return { ok: true, data } → END
shared_pattern: Both functions follow identical HTTP POST pattern: guard(hubUrl) → build endpoint → get timeout → build body → buildHubHeaders → fetch with AbortSignal → error/status branch → return {ok, data|error}
key_decisions: hubUrl missing → early return no_hub_url (both functions)
fetch network/timeout error → catch → return ok:false with err.message
HTTP non-2xx response → return ok:false with status code + truncated body text (max 400 chars)
HTTP success → parse JSON body → return ok:true with data

updateService....updates: arbitrary fields spread from updates object

important_notes: URL normalization: trailing slashes stripped from hubUrl before concatenation. Timeout enforced via AbortSignal.timeout(). Both functions are async, return Promise<{ok, data?, error?, status?}>. Exported as module.exports = { publishService, updateService }.
